# Rare-binary hunting by low host-spread (T1059)

**Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Surfaces rarely-deployed binaries — a common signal for attacker tooling, droppers, and LOLBins that appear on only one or two hosts. It lowercases the image name to fold case-variant duplicates, then computes `count_distinct(agent_hostname)` as host-spread alongside total executions per binary, and keeps only binaries seen on at most two hosts. Sorting ascending by host-spread then descending by exec count puts the rarest-but-busiest binaries at the top of the hunt list.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and actor_process_image_name != null and agent_hostname != null
| alter bin_lc = lowercase(actor_process_image_name)
| comp count_distinct(agent_hostname) as host_spread, count() as total_execs by bin_lc
| filter host_spread <= 2
| sort asc host_spread, desc total_execs
| limit 40
```
